_note: Hand-authored source of truth for the Star Map hook ring. Each entry is one harness lifecycle event (.claude/settings.json `hooks` key), in turn-chronological order, with plain-English copy for the dashboard tooltip. Keep in sync with .claude/settings.json — build.py folds this into GUILD.hooks.

hooks.label: You Send a Message
hooks.fires: The instant you send a prompt, before the guild reads it.

scripts.name: guild-routing-gate.sh
scripts.plain: Injects the Star Alliance routing rules into every message you send, so the Butler picks the right member and the right workflow before anyone acts. Proportional: small low-stakes turns get a ~150-token LITE reminder; campaign-grade or high-stakes turns get the full doctrine. Stakes always beats size; unknown → full. Lists live in data/harness.json policy; SA_GATE=full forces uniform.

hooks.label: Before Any Tool Runs
hooks.fires: Right before the guild fires any tool — a file edit, a command, a skill.

scripts.name: workflow-gate.py
scripts.plain: Blocks every tool until the turn has declared a star-map workflow banner. No banner, no action — this is the gate you keep seeing.

scripts.name: executor-enforce.py
scripts.plain: Holds the executor seat to MiniMax M3 across FOUR layers: (1) SPAWN — Task/Agent/Bash summon must resolve to model=minimax-m3, not haiku/glm/sonnet-as-doer; brain-tier Claude spawns pass. (2) BUTLER LOCK — on the MAIN session (CLAUDE_CODE_CHILD_SESSION unset), Edit/Write/MultiEdit/NotebookEdit are blocked entirely (subagents are exempt because they ARE the executor), Bash write commands (sed -i, cat >, rm, cp, mv, tee, touch, dd, echo >) are blocked, and MCP write-verb tools (create_/update_/delete_/insert_/drop_/truncate_/set_/put_/patch_/write_) are blocked. Read-only tools (Read/Glob/Grep/WebFetch/WebSearch/LS/TodoWrite) and MCP read-verb tools (get_/list_/fetch_/read_/find_/search_/query_/describe_/show_/view_/lookup_/select_) still pass through. The Butler can think, plan, and review — but it cannot mutate. (3) OVERRIDE CONTRACT — the bare SA_ALLOW_EXECUTOR token no longer grants bypass; the override MUST be paired with a real reason (≥15 chars) in the form `SA_ALLOW_EXECUTOR: <reason>`. The reason is logged to evolution/ledger.jsonl AND surfaced to the user as a systemMessage at the next UserPromptSubmit (no silent bypasses). (4) SCOREBOARD — evolution/scoreboard.py now shows an 'Executor Discipline' section: override count, override reasons, direct-write blocks, doer summons with minimax-m3 vs sonnet share, and a verdict (ALIGNED / MIXED / STRAYING). Kill switch: touch evolution/DISARMED or .claude/state/executor-enforce-disarmed. The /doer slash command (.claude/commands/doer.md) gives the agent a one-token path to the executor seat so the correct behavior is also the easy behavior.

scripts.name: high-alert.py
scripts.plain: When a skill, workflow, or agent is invoked, fires the alert banners that announce it (the ⚡ and 🗺 klaxons).

scripts.name: okf-gate.py
scripts.plain: Before any file write or edit, blocks knowledge documents that would be saved without proper Open-Knowledge-Format frontmatter (a `type:` field), so docs never drift out of format.

hooks.label: The Turn Ends
hooks.fires: When the guild finishes its reply and the turn closes.

scripts.name: turn-cost.py
scripts.plain: Records this turn's real Opus token cost and which routing tier fired (LITE vs FULL) to data/turn-cost.jsonl, so the efficiency report can prove the savings instead of estimating them.

scripts.name: turn-finalize.sh
scripts.plain: Once per turn (not per edit): regenerates member tables only if a loadout source changed, then coalesces ALL the turn's changes into ONE git commit — killing the per-file 'auto: Edit' storm. Commit only; never pushes.

scripts.name: workflow-banner-enforcer.py
scripts.plain: Checks that the finished turn declared a workflow and the right member reported for duty — even on plain-talk turns that fire no tools. Blocks the turn-end if either is missing.
